messages.text: Some of the buckets not opening properly causing weight issues, and issues with the upper product feeding sensors.
Shawn is adjusting the sensors in angle so they don’t get hit by the product, but still pointing at the center it seems to help a lot.

Pouch test check update ✅️ 


Upon checking the assorted pouch, we found the 34 pieces required by the product specifications. The print (best if used by) is printed, legible, and understandable as required by the spec.
My apologies. I had capper issues here in L1. Still need me?
7 and 10 still aren't running
I can come try to help. But fair warning, I have about 10 minutes of experience with the MFM interface. If it works like the older Yamato scales, I can probably figure it out
After reading more into it a lot of these issues are pointing towards needing a span calibration. It's pretty similar the HMI is just a little different
there a couple of things to try before a span calibration, but we can certainly do that and i can help.  I'll be over shortly
Machine is down Troy is here doing a calibration.
We have cleared the initial errors, but will need to do a span calibration, which requires specific weights to perform. I'm tracking those down.
Thank you Troy Baxter & Nathan Ruiz!
We'll create/look for a standard procedure to distribute and train to.
7 and 10 are now working again
Did a span calibration on those 2 buckets and that cleared the errors. I showed Nathan and Shawn how to do it. I'm happy to help, but trying to train as many as I can along the way.
Printer fault at 11:20am. Nathan is working on it

The Videojet printer shut down, and a fault code appeared saying the printhead needed cleaning. When we took it out, it was full of ink, and now we're cleaning it, Maintenance is looking into why it's leaking so much ink.
Running.
Ink jet needed an alignment. It was shooting off to the side creating a good size mess and faulting it out.

Nathan Ruiz Frederick Peairs Kimberly Morales I received some feedback from Yamato. The next time the HMI appears to glitch and is not displaying weights or sending the drop signal, go to Options and select Ignore Available Combinations. Please let me know if this resolves the issue the next time it occurs.

Pouch test check update ✅️ 


Upon checking the assorted pouch, we found the 34 pieces required by the product specifications. The print (best if used by) is printed, legible, and understandable as required by the spec.
Currently paused. We’ve got to sort the chocolate from the rework pouches to make sure we consume all the chocolate before lots code change

The Matrix stopped because it is ejecting the bags. The operator is working on the weight issue.
The MFM stopped for 5 minutes  and then resumed operation. One person was assigned to monitor the baskets to ensure proper performance.
The MFM stopped again at 12:44 am, due to a weight issue, causing continuous bag rejection. Maintenance was called.
The operator resolved the weight issue, and production resumed after a 10 minute downtime.
Maintenance is currently checking the baskets.

Update 3rd Shift: Product is within the specifications. 35 wrappers inside the pouch. Seal integrity of the pouch is in good condition. BBD and lot is confirmed to be correct/visible. No issues to report.
There’s 3 more pallets of V1 before switching to V2
The MFM was paused for 5 minutes, because it continues to eject bags. Maintenance was called

Hi team, just wondering if the "pitch error" message is normal, is over rejecting pouches with good weights due to this error. Angelica Perez Conner Boyd

I’ll come take a look at it Victor Mejia

With the speed we’re going it can happen if we’re not sorting fast enough.
Thanks Conner

Due to the speed of the line we have over accumulation due to weight and x ray. I would kindly suggest a pause while we pass all accumulation.


Angelica Perez Charity Young Conner Boyd
It's the safest way to get through. Please Conner Boyd Dakota Adair
We are having to many pouches fallin to the floor due to the same reason and EEs are also having over accumulation
Please, we need to address this. Conner Boyd Dakota Adair

We paused for 20 minutes within the hour to pass the accumulation from the check weigher. 
So the X-ray is built up from that pause, since Production can’t access the X-ray bin. 

The RR dictates that 97 pouches pass by the check weigher per minute. 

With one check weigher it is creating a bottleneck. 

We’ll looking into to adjusting the speed timing of the check weigher belt. 
Victor Mejia Dakota Adair Charity Young Angelica Perez
Thank you for the explanation, we hope this improves things.
We’ll pause to pass the accumulation, and make a decision on how to proceed from there.

The paused is counted from the start of the line, you would need to consider how long it takes to reach the pause by the end of the line and we are reaching over 20 pouches falling into the x-ray bin in a 10 minutes time frame
We'll need to consider a couple of thing since the speed was increased
Isn't it a problem with the sensitivity of the x-ray machine? Victor Mejia
Are the employees separating the bags, leaving some distance so they aren't rejected?
Could be
Yes but due to the speed is really hard to have a proper distance between bags
Why we are placing the pallets for rework? Victor Mejia
Wrong pallet, we should use PECO pallet instead of new pallets based on spec

The Z Conveyor Disconnect is complete. The intended purpose is to turn off the disconnect before unplugging the conveyor.  Turn the power back on after the conveyor plug is inspected to be dry and plugged in. Please have the operators trained in correct use, thank you.

Thank you, Chris Jeffs for help finishing the wire connection.

Curt Willis Justin Garner
Kitchen 3 Steam Pressure sensor check complete. Results: Both Passed. 
Notes: Before inspection the cables were not tight to the sensors. This could have been giving incomplete feed back. Fully seated when finished. The terminal ends are starting to green from not being fully seated so cable replacement may be required in the near future.
Tested at 30 and 90psi.
Both kettles are within 2psi of the analog gauges.

I want to make sure it is clear and everyone understands. This is not a new LOTO process. This switch was installed so the power to this 480 outlet can be turned off before anyone plugs or unplugs the conveyor to eliminate anyone getting electrocuted. Nathan Topham, will create an OPL so all teams can be trained.
We now have this new LOTO process Chris Jeffs and I have walked through it and we will follow up with our team as well
